channel.title: Obsidian Vault

item.title: Bayesian Regression
item.description:
tags: Statistics, Data Science, #ritvikmath
The notes are based on this video.Basic assumptions about the data in a regression setting: Comment, I am not so familiar with the second assumption. This is certainly not an OLS assumption. Maximize the probability by choosing a . Because is not related to , is the likelihood that we know in frequentist statistics. is the prior of the estimated coefficient.Notice that the pdf on the right of is , and .Using the most common prior
The will be explored and sampled by the HMC MCMC algorithm.
Therefore, back to ^c1f828The signs are flipped, so it's ARGMIN now. We get rid of the things that are unrelated to in and .Because is positive, it doesn't change when we multiply the things in with .At the end, replace the squared symbol with norm. Doesn't this look similar? When we re-write as , the way is estimated is the same as ridged regressions.Therefore, the variance of the prior distribution decides how much we punish overfitting. The smaller the variance is, the larger the penalty term will play a role. We can imagine that if we use a more certain prior, the evidence needed to sway the prior has to be even stronger than when we consider the prior to be uncertain (with a larger ).
